description: The bodily-injury counterpart to meridian-damage-assessment-v1 (which values property/vehicle loss). A bodily-injury exposure is valued differently from vehicle damage: not a single inspection estimate but a buildup of medical specials, future medical, lost wages, and general (non-economic) damages, reached by a medical/casualty method (records review, IME, demand package). Notarize-only audit record. The orchestrator carries evaluatedValue forward as the proposedAmount into meridian-authority-band-v1, so the same engine authority gate applies to injury settlements. Use this on the injury exposure of a multi-exposure claim (set exposureId); use damage-assessment on collision/property exposures. AGLedger records the valuation and its stated basis; it does not judge whether the evaluated value is correct.

exposureId.description: Optional exposure discriminator. One accident can have multiple exposures (e.g. own-vehicle collision + third-party property + bodily injury); set the same exposureId across the coverage/assessment/authority/settlement records of one exposure so N exposures coexist under one claimNumber.

claimantRole.description: Who is injured. Drives the coverage line (e.g. PIP/MedPay for first-party occupants vs bodily-injury liability for third parties).

injurySummary.description: Nature and extent of the injuries (the audit-relevant narrative).

evaluatedValue.description: Bottom-line evaluated claim value. The orchestrator carries this into the authority band as proposedAmount.

atMaximumMedicalImprovement.description: Optional. true once the claimant has reached MMI; if false, this valuation is interim and expected to be superseded as treatment completes.

evidenceRef.description: Pointer to the medical records / demand package in the system of record.
